refinement_sections.content: We need to create a way to check how well our credit risk prediction model works. It should not just fit the data we have but also work well on new data from different places.
The data includes customer details and their history of paying back loans. We need to know how many examples we have and if they are balanced across different regions.
The model should predict if a customer will pay back their loan based on their information. We want it to be accurate enough, but I am not sure what the exact accuracy percentage should be.

We need to think about how to test the model. Maybe we can split the data into training and testing sets, but I don't know how to do that properly. We should also look at different metrics like accuracy, but I am not sure which ones are best for this.

Overfitting means the model does too well on the training data but fails on new data. We need to find a way to avoid that, maybe by using techniques like cross-validation, but I need to learn more about that.
